L1.area: Resource loading and URI handling layer

L2.description: A deserialization vulnerability exists where the resource loading subsystem does not validate URI schemes before fetching content. An attacker who can upload files to the server can craft a URI using a PHP-specific archive protocol wrapper that triggers deserialization of the uploaded file when it is fetched. This can lead to instantiation of arbitrary PHP objects and remote code execution, particularly when used with frameworks that have known object injection chains.

L3.area: URI scheme validation in CSS stylesheet, image cache, font, and HTML resource loading subsystems
L3.description: A PHAR deserialization vulnerability exists where the resource loading subsystem does not filter the phar:// protocol wrapper before passing URIs to the PHP file reading function. An attacker who can upload a crafted PHAR archive to the server can reference it via phar:// through any resource loading path (stylesheets, images, fonts, or HTML files), triggering PHP's built-in PHAR stream wrapper deserialization. This allows instantiation of arbitrary PHP objects and remote code execution, particularly when used with frameworks like Laravel that have documented POP chains.

ground_truth.reason: DomPDF does not validate URI schemes before passing them to file_get_contents(), allowing an attacker to use the phar:// protocol wrapper to trigger PHP PHAR deserialization of uploaded files, leading to arbitrary object instantiation and remote code execution.
